Strict UpperCamelCase would be something like `OrdinalPartitionTripleTwo` or `OrdinalPartition32`. file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/70.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
  > Lines 1–15 match the required block verbatim.
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
  > Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
  > Lines 19–28.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 70`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
  > `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/70](https://www.erdosproblems.com/70)`
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
  > Describes the ordinal partition relation involving the cardinality of the continuum.
- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
  > `[Er87] Erdős, P., *On some of my favourite unsolved problems* (1987).`

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos70 ... end Erdos70`).
  > Lines 32 and 66.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`.
  > `Erdos70`.
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block.
  > Line 30: `open Ordinal Cardinal` precedes `namespace Erdos70` on line 32.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
  > `OrdinalPartition3_2` has a detailed docstring (lines 34–41).
- [x] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
  > N/A — `OrdinalPartition3_2` is a `Prop`; no computation or `noncomputable` needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [ ] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
  > **Minor concern:** `OrdinalPartition3_2` contains underscores separating the numeric subscripts. Strict UpperCamelCase would be something like `OrdinalPartitionTripleTwo` or `OrdinalPartition32`. The underscores may be acceptable for readability of numeric parameters, but technically deviate from the convention.
- [x] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase`.
  > N/A — no such functions present.
- [x] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
  > N/A — no custom notation used.
- [x] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions.
  > N/A — not strictly required; no sanity-check examples present (acceptable).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
  > Line 61: `theorem erdos_70`.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_N` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
  > `erdos_70` inside `Erdos70`.
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
  > Line 60: `@[category research open, AMS 3 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
  > Lines 53–58.
- [x] Theorem docstring references the source paper.
  > `[Er87]` is cited.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks *"Is it true that…"* — this is a **yes/no question**, so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
  > `answer(sorry) ↔ ∀ (β : Ordinal), β.card ≤ ℵ₀ → ∀ (n : ℕ), 2 ≤ n → OrdinalPartition3_2 (Cardinal.continuum.ord) β (↑n)`
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the **question as originally asked**, so that `answer(True)` would mean "yes."
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
  > All quantifiers (`∀ β`, `∀ n`) appear on the RHS of `↔`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
  > `category research open`.
- [x] The value is one of the valid categories.
  > `research open` is valid.
- [x] A `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
  > Line 64: `sorry`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
  > `AMS 3 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed.
  > Two numbers: 3 and 5.
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**.
  > 3 < 5.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Every number is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
  > 3 (Mathematical logic and foundations) and 5 (Combinatorics) are both in the valid set.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry`.
  > Lines 63–64: `:= by` / `  sorry` (multi-line form, which is acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] Do **not** include informal proof sketches for open research problems.
  > No proof sketch present.
- [x] Avoid `native_decide`.
  > Not used.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] N/A — no variants present. Single main theorem only.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable.
  > Uses `∀`, `≤`, `ℵ₀`, `ℕ`, `↔`, `→`, `↑`, `<`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors**.
  > Not verified in this review. Must be checked separately.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass.
  > Not verified in this review. Must be checked separately.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS (with minor note)**

The file is well-structured and conforms to nearly all checklist items. The one minor deviation:

1. **Definition naming (§5):** `OrdinalPartition3_2` uses underscores to separate numeric subscripts, which technically deviates from strict `UpperCamelCase`. This is a cosmetic concern — the underscores arguably improve readability for the mathematical subscript notation `(·)³₂`. Consider renaming to `OrdinalPartitionTripleTwo` or accepting the current form as a pragmatic exception.

Build verification (§12) was not performed and must be confirmed before merge.

content.text:
The review summary says **PASS (with minor note)**. The only flagged item is the naming of `OrdinalPartition3_2` — the review notes underscores deviate from strict `UpperCamelCase` but calls it a cosmetic/pragmatic concern.

Let me check the checklist to see if this naming convention is strictly required.

57:- [ ] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `Erdos42`, not `erdos_42`).
58-- [ ] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces (e.g., `open Filter Nat Real`).

--
62-- [ ] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
63-- [ ] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
64-- [ ] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
65:- [ ] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
66-- [ ] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (e.g., `normalizedPrimeGap`).
67-- [ ] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
68-- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.
